description:
Automated translation (refer to source for accuracy)

Keiko Kojima, chair of the Hanno City Food Life Improvement Leaders Council, has received the 2025 (Reiwa 7) Minister of Health, Labour and Welfare Award for Nutrition-Related Achievement and a Certificate of Recognition from the 55th Saitama Prefecture Health Promotion Gathering for Food Life Improvement Achievement. She has been dedicated to improving daily health habits—including salt reduction awareness—for over 40 years since joining in 1986.

Food Life Improvement Leaders (called "Shokukai-san") in Hanno work across 5 chapters and hold about 5 cooking classes per year, parent-child cooking workshops, and food education activities at city events. The group currently faces challenges with aging membership and declining new recruits.

In 2026 (Reiwa 8), a new training program for food life improvement leaders will be offered. Registered dietitians and health nurses will teach correct nutrition and health knowledge. Upon completion, participants will be registered as Food Life Improvement Leaders. Details will be announced later.

Source (Japanese): https://www.city.hanno.lg.jp/soshikikarasagasu/kenkohukusibu/hokencenter/8239.html
summary: ℹ️ Hanno Food Life Improvement Leaders Council
